findings.description: Text styling and emphasis are only partially preserved in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows more distinct heading emphasis and tighter typographic hierarchy for the title/subtitle and some table header text, while the HTML uses flatter web-font styling and lighter visual distinction. Content remains readable and identifiable.

findings.description: The HTML table header structure does not faithfully preserve the multi-row grouped headers from the PDF, causing column labels to be merged and distorted.
findings.evidence: PDF shows separate grouped headers: "2/14/2014" with sample IDs "AC04986 / AC04987 / AC04993", similarly for 2/17/2014, 2/26/2014, 2/27/2014, plus distinct columns for "Report #", "Dissolved", "PQL", and "Units\Hg 1631". HTML collapses these into single headers such as "Report # Dissolved Units\Hg 1631" and "2/14/2014 AC04986 AC04987 AC04993 Value" / "Qualifier", which is not the same column structure as the source.

findings.description: Several cell values and labels in the table are inaccurate or malformed compared with the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF row label reads "Al- Dissolved Aluminum"; HTML has "Al - Dissolved Aluminum" (minor formatting) but more importantly PDF shows the rightmost note spanning rows as "no NC standards for dissolved metals", while HTML splits it across two columns/rows as separate italicized fragments. PDF row label reads "B- Dissolved Barium" and "B- Dissolved Boron" conventions, but HTML includes "Ba- Dissolved Barium" and "B Dissolved" inconsistently. PDF has "Strontium Dissolved"; HTML has misspelled "Stronium Dissolved".

findings.description: There are OCR/transcription errors that alter text accuracy, including misspellings and malformed labels.
findings.evidence: HTML footer says "annlyzed" and "Reccomended" while PDF reads "analyzed" and "Recommended". HTML column header contains malformed text "Report # Dissolved Units\Hg 1631" rather than the distinct PDF labels. HTML row label "Stronium Dissolved" differs from PDF "Strontium Dissolved".

findings.description: Heading structure is acceptable in hierarchy but slightly over-interpreted relative to the PDF's centered title block.
findings.evidence: PDF presents centered title lines: "Data from Ash Release at Duke Energy Dan River Station", "Stormwater Discharge Upstream of Ash Pond - 009", and "Sampling Dates and Sample ID" as a title block above the table. HTML converts these into h1/h2/h3 headings, which is reasonable but imposes a semantic hierarchy not explicit in the PDF.
